items.headline: U.S. Stocks Decline for Third Straight Week Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Inflation Fears
items.summary: U.S. stock indexes concluded Friday, March 14, 2026, lower, marking their third consecutive week of losses. The market downturn was primarily driven by escalating geopolitical concerns stemming from the war with Iran, which continued to pressure oil prices higher, alongside persistent inflation worries and a downward revision of fourth-quarter GDP growth. Technology stocks led the declines, while defensive sectors like utilities and consumer staples showed resilience. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Down 0.61% for the day, closing lower.

items.headline: Ulta Beauty Slumps on Earnings Miss
items.summary: Ulta Beauty's stock fell sharply after its latest quarterly results missed analysts' profit targets.

items.headline: Adobe Falls Despite Revenue Beat
items.summary: Adobe shares declined, with investors reportedly underwhelmed by the company's forecast for recurring subscription revenue, despite beating Wall Street's sales and profit forecasts. News of CEO Shantanu Narayen stepping down also weighed on the stock.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Down on AI Model Delay
items.summary: Meta Platforms slid after reports indicated a delay in the launch of a new artificial intelligence model due to performance concerns.

items.headline: Salesforce Declines
items.summary: Salesforce shares experienced a daily decline.

items.headline: Boeing Posts Gains
items.summary: Boeing was among the strongest performers today.

items.headline: Charles Schwab Advances
items.summary: Financial services firm Charles Schwab saw gains today.

items.headline: UnitedHealth Rises
items.summary: UnitedHealth Group's stock moved higher today.

items.headline: Philip Morris International Up
items.summary: Philip Morris International saw an increase in its share price.

items.headline: Iran War Escalation Fuels Oil Price Surge, Rattling Markets
items.summary: The ongoing war with Iran continues to be a dominant factor, keeping pressure on global oil prices. Brent crude climbed back above $100 per barrel, rising 1.5% to $101.95, while U.S. crude oil rose 2.4% to $98.03. The conflict and Iran's actions have effectively halted cargo traffic through the crucial Strait of Hormuz, where a significant portion of the world's oil typically transits, intensifying fears of supply disruptions. This surge in energy costs threatens to fuel inflation and destabilize the global economy, contributing to a cautious market sentiment.

items.headline: Weak Economic Data Adds to Market Concerns
items.summary: Fresh economic data has added to investor unease. The U.S. Department of Commerce sharply revised down fourth-quarter GDP growth to an annual rate of 0.7%, indicating a significant slowdown. Additionally, fresh indicators pointed to weakening demand for durable goods and capital equipment. Consumer spending in January, adjusted for inflation, was anemic, and hiring has largely stagnated. Core inflation rose 3.1% year over year, the highest level in nearly two years, further pushing up the 10-year Treasury yield to 4.28%.

items.headline: Technology and Materials Lag, Utilities and Consumer Staples Lead
items.summary: Among S&P 500 sectors, technology stocks led declines, dropping 1.29%, while materials also lagged, falling 1.04%. Conversely, defensive sectors such as utilities and consumer staples posted gains, advancing 0.94% and 0.54%, respectively, demonstrating a flight to safety among investors. Financial services, health care, and consumer goods companies also saw some gains.

items.headline: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady Amid Inflationary Pressures
items.summary: Market participants are now focusing on next week's Federal Reserve policy meeting, where the central bank is widely expected to maintain interest rates at their current levels. However, some analysts suggest that rising oil prices and persistent inflation could lead some Fed officials to consider a rate hike, despite weaker GDP data.

items.headline: SanDisk and Ciena Among Top Weekly Tech Performers
items.summary: While the tech sector broadly declined today, SanDisk ($SNDK) surged 27% this week, becoming the best-performing tech stock on the S&P 500 due to reports of NAND memory drive shortages. Ciena ($CIEN) also performed strongly over the week, jumping approximately 15.5%, as a provider of adaptive networking systems.
